title: Foundever Strikes Deal With Creditors to Cut Debt Load
summary: Troubled business services giant Foundever Group has reached an agreement with a group of creditors to reduce its multi-billion dollar debt load, with the company’s controlling shareholder injecting fresh funds and installing new management.

title: Griffin Wants to Buy an Old Cottage Across From New Headquarters
summary: Ken Griffin has gone to extraordinary lengths to assemble parcels of land around his planned Citadel headquarters in Miami, including buying up all 138 units in a condo building across the street.

title: US Gasoline Prices Are Heading to $4 a Gallon Again
summary: It’s the middle of summer driving season, when Americans typically embark on road trips and burn through millions of barrels of gasoline each day. They’ll be paying more for every fill up.

title: Crop Markets Mixed on Shipping Worries, US-China Trade Concerns
summary: Crop markets were mixed Friday amid lingering tensions in the Black Sea and Strait of Hormuz, as well as concerns over US trade ties with China.

title: Anglo Selects Penny’s Group as Preferred Bidder for De Beers
summary: Anglo American Plc has selected a group led by De Beers’ former boss Gareth Penny as the preferred bidder for its sale of the world’s biggest diamond mining company, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: CoreLogic Tweaks Terms on Struggling Debt Sale, Boosts Loan
summary: CoreLogic Inc. amended the terms of its roughly $5.3 billion debt offering and shifted part of the funding to loans from bonds after struggling with lukewarm investor demand.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
summary: Oil surged on growing fears the US and Iran could return to full war, threatening flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Chip Stocks Poised for Bear Market in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers gathered pace on Friday, driving the group that has led this year’s stock rally toward a bear market on worries that the artificial-intelligence spending spree is becoming harder to justify.

title: How New PM Andy Burnham Affects Your Money and UK Markets: Live Q&A
summary: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about the UK’s new prime minister Andy Burnham and what his policies mean for your money and markets in a Live Q&A on Tuesday, July 16 at 2 p.m. BST.

title: Wild Weather Disrupts Copper Mines and Ports in Central Chile
summary: Copper mines and ports in central Chile curtailed some operations in the face of a powerful winter storm, while major mining installations in the north remained unaffected.

title: Hedge Fund Blasts Settlement With SEC After ‘Gag Rule’ Lifted
summary: Connecticut hedge fund Deccan Value Investors is speaking out about its 2022 settlement of Securities and Exchange Commission claims that it breached its fiduciary duty to two university endowments after the regulator recently lifted its so-called gag rule.

title: Chips Stocks Sink Into Bear Market as 105% AI Rally Fizzles
summary: A closely-watched index of semiconductor stocks is set to fall into a bear market, unwinding a chunk of the blistering memory-led rally that saw the gauge more than double in just three months.

title: Dollar Hedging Costs Sink to Their Lowest Level This Year
summary: The cost of hedging against swings in the dollar has fallen to its lowest level this year, signaling that traders see little chance of a major catalyst disrupting the world’s reserve currency despite an uncertain Federal Reserve outlook and resurgent conflict in the Middle East.

title: Gold Heads for Second Weekly Drop as War Raises Fed Hike Fears
summary: Gold was on track for the biggest weekly loss since early June as renewed hostilities in the Middle East raised concerns that higher interest rates will be needed to contain war-driven inflation.

title: BASF Seeks Banks to Lead IPO of €20 Billion Agrichemical Unit
summary: BASF SE has invited banks to pitch for roles on an initial public offering of its agrichemical division in Frankfurt, in what could be one of the largest offerings the bourse has seen in recent years.

title: US Stocks Plunge as Concerns of ‘DeepSeek 2.0’ Hit Chipmakers
summary: US stocks plunged on Friday as the selloff in semiconductor names gathered steam following the reveal of an artificial-intelligence model from a Chinese startup.

title: Hyperscalers Drag Down Bond Gauges Across Global Markets
summary: The bonds sold by hyperscalers to fuel their artificial intelligence ambitions have become a drag on investor portfolios from London to Tokyo.

title: Neglected Quant Safety Trade Makes a Comeback as AI Risks Rise
summary: As worries build around the artificial intelligence trade, investors are starting to seek shelter in a more pedestrian corner of the stock market: companies with sturdy finances.

title: IBM’s Warning Is a ‘Hammer’ Slamming Down on Tech’s AI Outsiders
summary: International Business Machines Corp.’s warning this week that its sales are falling well short of expectations highlights a growing divide between artificial intelligence winners and everyone else in the tech universe.

title: Apple Tops Nvidia as World’s Largest Company Amid Tech Rotation
summary: Apple Inc. is again the biggest company in the world after wresting the title from Nvidia Corp., which has held it since May 2025.
